learningObjectives.title: Understand SAP TM business documents
learningObjectives.description: Explain key documents used for transportation planning, execution, and settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Understand end-to-end document flow
learningObjectives.description: Describe document progression from source demand to freight settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate planning and execution documents
learningObjectives.description: Clarify responsibilities of freight units, freight orders, and bookings.

sections.content: Transportation Execution in SAP TM involves multiple business documents that work together throughout the logistics lifecycle. Each document has a specific responsibility, beginning with transportation demand and ending with freight settlement. Understanding the relationship between these documents is essential for both SAP TM implementation and certification.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: SAP TM uses different business documents for planning, execution and settlement. Each document serves a unique purpose in the transportation process.

A customer places an order for machinery. SAP S/4HANA creates an Outbound Delivery, SAP TM generates a Freight Unit, the optimizer creates a Freight Order, the shipment is executed, Proof of Delivery is received and finally a Freight Settlement Document is generated to pay the carrier.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain document consistency throughout the transportation lifecycle. Avoid manual document creation unless required for exceptional business scenarios.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational logistics company processes over 40,000 Freight Orders daily. Every document is automatically linked from Freight Unit creation through Freight Settlement, providing complete end-to-end transportation traceability.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Certification exams commonly ask the sequence of SAP TM documents. Remember: Delivery -> Freight Unit -> Freight Order/Freight Booking -> Freight Settlement Document.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Freight Booking with Freight Settlement. Freight Booking reserves transportation capacity, whereas Freight Settlement calculates and pays transportation charges.

items: Proof of Delivery confirms successful shipment completion.
Freight Settlement Document is generated after execution for carrier payment.
